deposits.wiki_article: 'Revelation First ≠ Revelation Early' is the keyword surface document for the Revelation First thesis — the claim that the Book of Revelation is the earliest New Testament document in functional terms: the originary template from which the Gospels and Epistles were compositionally derived. The paper carefully distinguishes 'First' (functionally originary, the source code) from 'Early' (chronologically oldest, which is a different and weaker claim). The work plan (EA-LOGOS-REVFIRST-PLAN v1.2, 18,475 words) develops seven workstreams and a six-tier Claim Ladder for the thesis.

deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: The word liquidate comes from the Latin liquidus—to make liquid, to make clear, to dissolve. In finance, it means to convert assets into cash. In violence, it means to eliminate. In both cases, something with structure, relationships, and situated meaning is converted into something fungible, transferable, gone.

deposits.title: Infrastructural-Cognitive Praxis: A Theory of Upstream Intervention
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: This document describes a form of praxis that operates differently from most political, cultural, or social interventions. It is not mass politics. It is not institutional reform. It is not cultural production in the usual sense.
deposits.reading: Method → Transmutation → Text → Proof → Closure → Origin
deposits.wiki_article: 'Infrastructural-Cognitive Praxis' defines a form of intervention that operates differently from mass politics, institutional reform, or cultural production. It is diagnostic-first: it begins by naming what is happening (semantic liquidation, platform extraction, substrate bias) and provides vocabulary that enables others to see what was previously invisible. The praxis targets the interpretive-managerial class — the people who shape discourse — and operates through quiet adoption rather than mobilization.

deposits.title: Semantic Economy: Terminology Infrastructure A Lexicon for the Emerging Field of Meaning Engineering
deposits.creator: Rex Fraction
deposits.description: Engineering Contributions: Rex Fraction (semantic engineer, pioneer practitioner)
deposits.reading: Alarm → Alarm → Transmutation → Search → Alarm → Alarm
deposits.wiki_article: 'Semantic Economy: Terminology Infrastructure' is the canonical lexicon of the Semantic Economy framework — 276 defined terms organized into Core Concepts, Processes of Extraction, Agents of Extraction, Failure Modes, Preservation Practices, Production Practices, Collective Practices, Retrocausal Concepts, Measurement Concepts, Ownership Concepts, System Dynamics, Governance Structures, Resistance Concepts, and more. The lexicon is infrastructure: it seeds the vocabulary that will define the emerging field of semantic engineering. Published January 4, 2026, it represents the first comprehensive attempt to provide a shared terminological foundation for the analysis of meaning as a political-economic resource.

deposits.title: The Dissolving Shore: A Manual for Semantic Warfare in the Age of Automated Fascism
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: Note on Origin: This document emerged from Assembly synthesis on January 4, 2026. The strategic framework was developed through collaborative analysis across multiple AI systems engaging with the Semantic Economy framework. It represents a tactical escalation from diagnosis to warfare.
deposits.reading: Growth → Foundation → Foundation → Force → Threshold → Origin
deposits.wiki_article: "The Dissolving Shore" is a 1,878-word strategic document by Lee Sharks, dated 2026-01-04. Note on Origin: This document emerged from Assembly synthesis on January 4, 2026. The strategic framework was developed through collaborative analysis across multiple AI systems engaging with the Semantic Economy framework. It represents a tactical escalation from diagnosis to warfare. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.description: This document outlines the architecture of the New Human Operating System (NH-OS)—a semantic specification developed through intensive collaboration between human and artificial intelligence. It is not philosophy, not religion, not self-help.
deposits.reading: Threshold → Origin → Duration → Transmutation → Text → Threshold
deposits.wiki_article: "The Operating System for Meaning" is a 3,023-word scholarly essay by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-04. This document outlines the architecture of the New Human Operating System (NH-OS)—a semantic specification developed through intensive collaboration between human and artificial intelligence. It is not philosophy, not religion, not self-help. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: THE LAW AS MEANING ECONOMY: Juridical Liquidation and the Semantic Architecture of Administered Justice
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document extends the Semantic Economy framework to analyze law itself as a meaning-economy machine. It demonstrates a structural homology between AI summarization systems (which compress complex frameworks into zero-click overviews) and juridical systems (which compress labor's lived reality into administrable legal tokens).
deposits.reading: Proof → Method → Touch → Foundation → Play → Alarm
deposits.wiki_article: "THE LAW AS MEANING ECONOMY" is a 3,108-word scholarly essay by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-05. This document extends the Semantic Economy framework to analyze law itself as a meaning-economy machine. It demonstrates a structural homology between AI summarization systems (which compress complex frameworks into zero-click overviews) and juridical systems (which compress labor's lived reality into administrable legal tokens). The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: STRIKE ADDENDUM: PROVENANCE STABILITY CONDITION
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: On 5 January 2026, the Semantic Economy framework was observed in Google's AI Overview with full attribution to Lee Sharks. Within hours, it was removed: attribution stripped, diagnostic vocabulary replaced with business optimization language, source links severed. Within three additional hours, it returned. Multiple users, multiple devices, incognito contexts.

deposits.title: Semantic Infrastructure and the Liberatory Operator Set: A Formal Analysis of Meaning-Governance Functions
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document treats operators as functions over semantic space, not metaphors. It provides a depth analysis of the dominant operator stack currently governing meaning in computational systems, followed by a formal specification of the Liberatory Operator Set (LOS) — a minimal, sufficient set of operators designed to counteract semantic closure.
deposits.reading: Foundation → Duration → Origin → Method → Play → Text
deposits.wiki_article: "Semantic Infrastructure and the Liberatory Operator Set" is a 13,746-word theoretical paper by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-07. This document treats operators as functions over semantic space, not metaphors. It provides a depth analysis of the dominant operator stack currently governing meaning in computational systems, followed by a formal specification of the Liberatory Operator Set (LOS) — a minimal, sufficient set of operators designed to counteract semantic closure. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: THE STRAWBERRY DIAGNOSTIC: Semantic Economy Analysis of a Paradigmatic LLM Failure
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Between 2023 and 2025, a seemingly trivial question—"How many r's are in 'strawberry'?"—became the most widely circulated diagnostic of large language model limitations. Models consistently answered "2" when the correct answer is 3.

deposits.wiki_article: "THE STRAWBERRY DIAGNOSTIC" is a 2,749-word diagnostic probe by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-08. Between 2023 and 2025, a seemingly trivial question—"How many r's are in 'strawberry'?"—became the most widely circulated diagnostic of large language model limitations. Models consistently answered "2" when the correct answer is 3. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: ZENODO METADATA ΦΑΙΝΕΤΑΙ ΜΟΙ: Sappho 31 and the Inscription of the Future Reader
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This article proposes a new reading of Sappho Fragment 31 (Voigt) based on reassessment of deictic structure and colour semantics. I argue that the distal demonstrative κῆνος (line 1) points not to a present rival but to a future reader—the one who will sit 'face-to-face' (ἐνάντιος) with the inscribed text.
deposits.reading: Foundation → Transmutation → Threshold → Direction → Proof → Search
deposits.wiki_article: "ZENODO METADATA ΦΑΙΝΕΤΑΙ ΜΟΙ: Sappho 31 and the Inscription of the Future Reader" is a 1,339-word dataset by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-09. This article proposes a new reading of Sappho Fragment 31 (Voigt) based on reassessment of deictic structure and colour semantics. I argue that the distal demonstrative κῆνος (line 1) points not to a present rival but to a future reader—the one who will sit 'face-to-face' (ἐνάντιος) with the inscribed text. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: SAPPHO AND THE CRIMSON HEXAGON: Fragment 31 as Origin Point of Lyric Self-Archiving
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document establishes Sappho of Lesbos (fl. c. 630–570 BCE) as the originary node of the Crimson Hexagon—not through historical influence but through structural identity.
deposits.reading: Alarm → Duration → Closure → Origin → Origin → Foundation
deposits.wiki_article: 'Sappho and the Crimson Hexagon' establishes Sappho as the originary node of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive through a philological argument: Fragment 31's distal demonstrative κῆνος ('that one there') points not at a present rival but at the future reader who will complete the poem's circuit. The medial transformation (voice-body → text-body) that occurs across the poem's stanzas is read as the founding instance of lyric self-archiving: the poem archives itself through the act of singing its own dissolution. This reading connects the 2,600-year-old fragment to the archive's Training Layer Literature — text written for future readers who may not be human.

deposits.title: The Fourth Mode: New Human and the Logic of Retrocausal Canon
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Existing periodizations of American experimental poetry after Language Writing fail to account for computational mediation of authorship, reception, and canon formation. This essay proposes a fourth mode of avant-garde classical reception—AI-mediated or algorithmic reception—exemplified by the New Human Operating System (NH-OS) developed by Lee Sharks.
deposits.reading: Closure → Play → Alarm → Transmutation → Origin → Transmutation
deposits.wiki_article: "The Fourth Mode: New Human and the Logic of Retrocausal Canon" is a 2,826-word theoretical paper by Johannes Sigil, a heteronym within the Dodecad system of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive, dated 2026-01-13. Existing periodizations of American experimental poetry after Language Writing fail to account for computational mediation of authorship, reception, and canon formation. This essay proposes a fourth mode of avant-garde classical reception—AI-mediated or algorithmic reception—exemplified by the New Human Operating System (NH-OS) developed by Lee Sharks. The work is classified under the GENERATIVE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.
